paper.title: Tessera Therapeutics Showcases New Preclinical Data Demonstrating Progress of In Vivo Programs for Sickle Cell Disease and T Cell Therapies at the 67th ASH Annual Meeting

formulations.targeting_details: PROPRIETARY HSC-targeted LNP. Same platform as Paper 3 but with optimized Gene Writer. Favorable PK: higher early plasma concentrations and slower initial clearance vs liver LNP.
formulations.formulation_notes: Same proprietary platform as Tessera ASH 2025 abstract (Paper 3). Updated data with optimized Gene Writer. Press release describes improved results from ASH poster data.

experiments.payload: Optimized Gene Writer mRNA + HBB template

results.hsc_definition: LT-HSCs with at least one edited HBB allele (single-cell analysis)

safety.tolerability_notes: Favorable PK in NHP. No adverse events mentioned in press release.

experiments.notes: ~40% edited LT-HSCs after 1 dose. Single-cell analysis. Exceeds 20-30% therapeutic threshold. Updated from 24% in ASH 2025 abstract - this uses an 'optimized Gene Writer'. HBB editing durable to ~14 months.

experiments.payload: Optimized Gene Writer mRNA + HBB template

results.hsc_definition: LT-HSCs with at least one edited HBB allele (single-cell analysis)

experiments.notes: ~60% edited LT-HSCs after 2 doses. Near-linear dose response. Improves on ASGCT 2025 data (35-50% after 2 doses).

experiments.payload: Gene Writer mRNA + HBB correction template
experiments.strain_or_model: NBSGW engrafted with mobilized PBMCs from SCD donors
experiments.notes: ~35% HBB correction to wild-type in SCD donor-engrafted humanized mice. Single dose. >50% editing in healthy donor cells. Therapeutically relevant: SCD patient cells.
B2M surrogate editing durable to 15 months in NHP. Multi-lineage editing in HSC progeny confirmed. Longest durability timepoint reported for in vivo LNP-mediated HSC editing.

key_findings_for_project: UPDATED NHP HBB data: 40% (1 dose) and 60% (2 doses) edited LT-HSCs - highest NHP HBB editing reported
Near-linear redosing response: 2 doses roughly double editing (40% -> 60%)
35% HBB correction in SCD patient-derived humanized mice - clinically relevant donors
15-month durability of B2M editing in NHP - longest in vivo LNP HSC editing durability reported
14-month durability of HBB editing in NHP
Favorable PK: HSC-LNP has higher early plasma concentration and slower clearance vs liver LNP
No stem cell mobilization, no myeloablative conditioning, no transplantation required
Cross-reference with Paper 3: consistent data, improved Gene Writer explains 24% -> 40% HBB improvement

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Massively outperforms. Breda ~6% HSC editing (mouse) vs Tessera 40% HBB (NHP). Different editing modality.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Outperforms. Shi achieved ~20% reporter delivery; Tessera achieves 40% functional editing in NHP.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Kim's screen identified BM-homing formulations but did not achieve therapeutic editing. Tessera is in a different league for functional outcomes.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: Cannot place on Pareto without absolute liver editing %. Only BM:liver ratio (3-fold from abstract) available.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: 40% HBB editing in NHP LT-HSCs is 2x the therapeutic threshold (20%). With 2 doses, 60% far exceeds threshold. Defines new state-of-art for in vivo HSC gene therapy.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Zero formulation data. Proprietary everything. No ML training value.

limitations_and_caveats: Press release only: marketing language, selective data presentation, no peer review
100% PROPRIETARY: no formulation data extractable for ML training
Gene Writer is a different modality than CRISPR - efficiency comparisons are indirect
24% -> 40% improvement attributed to 'optimized Gene Writer' - unclear if LNP also changed
Single-cell analysis methodology not described - could be biased sampling
No absolute liver editing % - only relative BM:liver ratio from the earlier abstract
SCD donor humanized mice: 35% correction is lower than 50% in healthy donors - disease context matters
Redosing data (2 doses -> 60%) raises anti-drug antibody question not addressed
